Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The user wants us to generate 20 open-ended, thought-provoking questions that delve deeper into the rationale behind the process flows, potential improvements, risk management, decision-making, stakeholder communication, and performance measurement. No SQL queries. Just list the questions.

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: The prompt includes a pseudo-BPMN process description. We need to produce 20 open-ended questions that reflect deeper aspects: rationale behind the process flows, potential improvements, risk management, decision-making, stakeholder communication, performance measurement.

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We need to ensure each question is open-ended, not yes/no. They can be like What are the key factors influencing etc.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to consider each major stage: market research, R&D, component sourcing, shipping, assembly, quality checks, packing, distribution, inventory monitoring, adjusting orders, etc.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Potential questions around risk: supply chain disruptions, geopolitical risk, component shortage, quality control, logistics, customs, regulatory compliance, inventory, demand forecasting.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Potential questions about process: rationale for parallel gateway for component sourcing vs sequential? rationale for 24/7 assembly? rationale for separate air freight vs cargo ship? Why separate distribution paths? How to coordinate between regional distribution centers? How to handle VIP/pre-orders? How to monitor inventory and sales forecast? How to adjust orders to suppliers based on demand changes? How to communicate with suppliers? How to ensure compliance across markets? How to manage cost vs speed? What metrics are used for performance measurement? How to improve process? What improvements could be made?

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Open-ended question style: What are the main challenges...? How might...? What could be the implications...? What criteria...? How could...? Why do we...? What would happen if...? How can we ensure...? What trade-offs exist...? etc.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We must produce exactly 20 questions. Not more, not less.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Well make them varied across categories. Make sure they are clear and thought-provoking. We must not include any SQL queries.

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Potential structure: 1. Rationale behind process flows 2. Potential improvements 3. Risk management 4. Decision-making 5. Stakeholder communication 6. Performance measurement Make sure each question covers one or more categories. List them as numbered list. Ok. Now we answer.
